summary: Stancil argues that AI’s impact on analytics will shift from making better dashboards to absorbing the qualitative, contextual knowledge living in people’s heads. His experiment with AI “analysts” shows that insight depends on prior context, suggesting the most valuable tools will capture human context rather than just automate quantitative analysis.
money_quotes: Perhaps human intuition is just a large context window and a creative RAG process.
Insight isn't the data; it is what's not in our heads.
I quickly found myself spending more time—and getting more return from—the contextual prompting than I did on asking quantitative questions and looking at the results.
But at some point, the vampire squids will run out of books to read, and turn to our heads for new sources of text.
And when we become a data tool, we become BI too.
key_insight: The next phase of BI will be driven less by automated charting and more by capturing human context, because that missing context is what turns data into insight.
